feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Light grey background with centered bolded headline (25% frame height) in dark grey. Below: clinical line graph with teal (progesterone) and red dotted (estradiol) curves spanning childhood through menopause, with perimenopausal phase highlighted in green box. Graph sourced citation in small grey text at bottom. Minimal overlays, clean scientific presentation.
posts.angle: Progesterone's drop in perimenopause is the first major shift — and it changes everything from inflammation to mood.
posts.build_brief:
**Format**: Square carousel, 3 slides (1080px canvas).

**Slide 1 — Hook & Headline**:
- Background: warm cream (#F5F1EB or similar soft neutral, complements EHN green #39B54A without competing).
- Centred, bold headline spanning ~25% of frame height at top third: "Perimenopause" in Satoshi Bold (95–110px equivalent), with the word "Progesterone" in EHN green #39B54A italic.
- Below headline, small supporting line in Satoshi Medium, charcoal: "The hormone shift that rewires everything" (~50px relative weight, warm charcoal).
- Whitespace-heavy, minimal overlay, clinical authority register.

**Slide 2 — Clinical Line Graph**:
- Same cream background.
- Centred line graph illustrating two curves across lifespan (childhood → menopause):
  - **Progesterone line**: solid EHN green #39B54A (primary visual anchor, ~3–4px stroke).
  - **Estradiol line**: soft sage or muted grey-green dotted line (harmonises with green, not competing).
  - X-axis labels: "Childhood / Reproductive / Perimenopause / Menopause" (small Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~40px equiv).
  - Perimenopause phase: subtle box highlight in soft sage or very pale green (not bold, suggests rather than shouts).
- Graph footnote: tiny grey citation line at bottom (e.g. "Data: adapted from clinical literature on hormone trajectories").
- Clean, minimal overlays; scientific presentation intact.

**Slide 3 — Three Key Functions**:
- Background: cream, same as Slide 1.
- Headline: "Why it matters" in Satoshi Bold (~85px), with "matters" in EHN green #39B54A italic.
- Three short statements below (Satoshi Medium, ~50px relative, stacked vertically, breathing space between):
  1. "🔥 *Progesterone reduces inflammation* — its decline amplifies joint, gut & skin symptoms"
  2. "😊 *Anti-anxiety hormone* — fewer molecules = heightened worry, racing thoughts"
  3. "⏬ *Keeps histamine in check* — when it drops, mast cells activate (hives, migraines, flushes)"
- Whitespace-generous, easy to read, clinical tone.

**Colour harmony**: EHN green #39B54A as the primary anchor (progesterone curve + accent words). Cream background (warm, clinical, calm). Sage/muted grey-green accents (estradiol curve, perimenopause box highlight) that sit in the same tonal family as green, never clash. Charcoal type for maximum readability.

**Typography lock**: All headlines Satoshi Bold (700), one accent word in green italic. Body/supporting Satoshi Medium (500). No point sizes <36px on card; body >=40px relative weight.

**Closure**: Small "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium at bottom of Slide 3, green close line.
posts.design_recipe:
**Reusable Carousel Template (3-Slide Green-Anchored Clinical Authority)**

**Canvas**: 1080px square carousel, 3 slides.

**Colour Recipe** (always anchored to EHN green #39B54A):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (headline accent, primary data line, close line).
- Background: Warm cream or soft white (clinical calm, non-competing).
- Secondary harmony: Sage, soft grey-green, or muted blue-grey (data accents, supporting elements; always in same tonal family as green, never clash).
- Typography: Charcoal or dark grey for all body text (legibility floor).

**Slide 1 — Hook & Headline**:
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95–110px equiv): "[Main concept]" + one meaningful word in green italic.
- Supporting line (Satoshi Medium, ~50px relative): "[Subheading/hook line]".
- Composition: Headline at upper third, supporting line below, generous whitespace. Cream background.

**Slide 2 — Visual Data/Graph**:
- Central line graph, chart, or illustration spanning ~60% of frame.
- Primary line/data in EHN green #39B54A (solid stroke, ~3–4px).
- Secondary line/data in sage or muted grey-green (solid or dotted, harmonises with green).
- Axis labels in Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~40px relative.
- Optional: subtle highlight box (very pale sage/green) for emphasis area.
- Citation/source line: tiny Satoshi Medium, light grey, bottom corner.
- Composition: Whitespace-heavy, minimal overlay, scientific register.

**Slide 3 — Three Supporting Points**:
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~85px): "[Concept]" + one word in green italic.
- Three short points (Satoshi Medium, ~50px relative) stacked vertically with breathing space.
- Optional: emoji or small icon per point (adds warmth without clutter).
- Composition: Left-aligned or centred, generous leading, cream background.

**Closure**: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, green, bottom of final slide).

**Overall feel**: Clinical authority, warm and grounded, EHN-green-anchored, Australian natural palette (cream, sage, charcoal tones). Never stock-wellness clichés, never sub-36px type. Layout is whitespace-generous and typographically clear.
posts.on_image:
**Slide 1**: "Perimenopause *Progesterone* | The hormone shift that rewires everything"

**Slide 2**: [Line graph: green solid curve (Progesterone: Childhood → Reproductive → Perimenopause decline → Menopause); sage dotted curve (Estradiol trajectory). X-axis: Childhood / Reproductive / Perimenopause / Menopause. Perimenopause phase marked with subtle pale-sage box. Footer: "Data: adapted from clinical literature on hormone trajectories"]

**Slide 3**: "Why it *matters* | 🔥 Progesterone reduces inflammation — its decline amplifies joint, gut & skin symptoms | 😊 Anti-anxiety hormone — fewer molecules = heightened worry, racing thoughts | ⏬ Keeps histamine in check — when it drops, mast cells activate (hives, migraines, flushes)"

**Branding**: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line, bottom of Slide 3)
posts.caption:
Perimenopause doesn't start with hot flushes — it starts with progesterone. 

In our 30s and 40s, progesterone is the quiet hero: it buffers inflammation, calms the nervous system, and keeps histamine (the itch-and-flush hormone) under control. When progesterone begins its steep decline in perimenopause — sometimes years before your period stops — those protective jobs start slipping away.

That's why so many women in their mid-40s suddenly notice joint stiffness, digestive flare-ups, racing thoughts at 3am, or hives they've never had before. It's not new stress or getting older. It's progesterone leaving the building.

If this resonates, a functional-medicine approach to perimenopause — looking at your full hormone picture, nutrient status, and nervous-system support — can make a real difference. We work through this with blood work, symptom tracking, and evidence-based nutrition and lifestyle strategies.

Ready to get a clearer picture of what's driving your symptoms? Book a consultation to explore your hormonal health.

#perimenopause #hormonehealth #womenshealth #functionalnutrition #progesterone #Adelaide
posts.gmb_caption: Perimenopause starts with progesterone — and we help you understand your shifting hormones. Book an appointment in Eastwood, Adelaide to explore what's happening in your body and how targeted nutrition and lifestyle changes can ease the transition.

posts.visual_recipe: Split-screen layout with black-and-white headshot portrait on left (bearded man with glasses, ~40% width) and bright blue background on right with white text hierarchy: branded logo/icon at top, "PODCAST 59" in circle badge, episode title "Gut Parasites Part 1" in large sans-serif, and presenter credits below in smaller font. Clean, professional podcast thumbnail style with high contrast between portrait and solid blue field.
posts.angle: SIBO and gas: why your bloating might not be what you think it is.
posts.build_brief: Canvas: 1080 × 1080px square card. Layout: Split-screen composition (mirroring the winner's left-portrait / right-text hierarchy). LEFT SIDE (~40% width): cream (#F5F1ED) background featuring a grounded editorial illustration or soft photograph—a clinician's hand holding a digestive-system diagram or a calm close-up of herbs/gut-health imagery in naturalistic tones (sage, warm grey, muted terracotta). RIGHT SIDE (~60% width): solid sage-green background (#39B54A primary anchor, or a softer complementary teal/soft grey-blue that harmonizes with the green). Stack text hierarchy top-to-bottom: (1) Small EHN logo lockup (unobtrusive, top-left of right field, ~40px height); (2) Circular badge or label "FUNCTIONAL DEEP-DIVE" in off-white text on green background (~80px diameter, Satoshi Bold, upper-right area); (3) Headline "SIBO *unmasked*" in large Satoshi Bold (~120px), one word (unmasked) in cream or charcoal italic for accent; (4) Short supporting line "What your symptoms are *really* telling you" (~48px, Satoshi Medium, warm tone); (5) Clinician credit + location line at bottom in small (~36px) Satoshi Medium, off-white. Whitespace: generous left margin on right field, balanced visual weight between portrait and text field. Border radius: 12–16px, soft card edge. Overall mood: clean, clinical, editorial—high contrast between portrait warmth and solid green field, professional podcast-thumbnail aesthetic but rendered in EHN's green-grounded palette.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE — Topic-Agnostic Shell:

Canvas: 1080 × 1080px square card.

Layout: Split-screen composition with two equal-visual-weight fields.

LEFT FIELD (~40% width): Cream or warm neutral background (#F5F1ED or equivalent) featuring a grounded editorial illustration, soft photograph, or clinician-hands shot. Imagery in naturalistic tones (sage, muted terracotta, soft grey, warm charcoal). Border radius 12–16px on card edge.

RIGHT FIELD (~60% width): Solid complementary colour anchored to EHN green (#39B54A). Secondary colour chosen to harmonize: soft teal, sage variation, muted grey-blue, or warm charcoal—never pure black, never a clashing accent. Text stacked top-to-bottom with clean whitespace:

1. Logo lockup (unobtrusive, top-left corner, ~40px height, white or off-white).
2. Circular or pill-shaped badge label in Satoshi Bold, ~80px diameter, white text on green/complementary background (e.g., "FUNCTIONAL DEEP-DIVE", "CLINIC INSIGHT", "[TOPIC] EXPLAINED").
3. Headline in Satoshi Bold (~120px, relative scale), ONE meaningful word in complementary italic accent (cream, soft grey, or a tonal highlight) to draw eye.
4. Supporting subline in Satoshi Medium (~48px, relative scale), warm or readable tone, short phrase.
5. Credit/clinician line + location in small Satoshi Medium (~36px, relative scale), off-white or neutral.

Visual hierarchy: High contrast between portrait warmth and solid field colour; generous left margin on right field; professional, podcast-thumbnail aesthetic. EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor throughout; secondary palette harmonizes, never clashes. Card branding footer: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green or complementary colour, Satoshi Bold, ~32px).

Typography lock: Satoshi Bold (headlines), Satoshi Medium (body/supporting). Never sub-36px text on card.

Legibility floor: Headline ≥~95px, supporting line ≥~48px, small text ≥36px on 1080 canvas. Shorten copy, never shrink font.

posts.caption:
SIBO gets blamed for a lot—but bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort can have several drivers. If you've been told you have Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth, it's worth understanding what's *actually* happening in your gut, and whether the treatment plan matches your symptoms.

In functional medicine, we dig deeper than the diagnosis. We look at the underlying causes—food sensitivities, motility, stomach acid, inflammation, even stress. That's where real, lasting change begins.

If you've had a SIBO diagnosis and treatment hasn't shifted things, there might be more to explore. Let's chat about what's really going on.

#SIBOawareness #GutHealth #FunctionalMedicine #FoodSensitivity #AdamaHealth
posts.gmb_caption: SIBO can be confusing—and treatment doesn't always stick. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we investigate the root causes of your bloating and gas, not just the label. Book a consultation to explore what's really driving your symptoms.

posts.visual_recipe: Screenshot of a news article/TV broadcast layered with overlaid white sans-serif headline text ("AN AUSTRALIAN SCIENTIST JUST WENT VIRAL IN AMERICA") positioned center-frame in bold, uppercase font. A black rounded banner bar with white text ("EPISODE 9 OF AN APPLE A DAY SERIES") sits mid-frame. The background shows a blurred newscast interface with a headshot of a man in a maroon shirt on the right side and article text on the left discussing an apple-derived health business. The composition uses contrast between the bold white text overlay and the muted gray/blurred background photo.
posts.angle: How a functional-medicine shift to intermittent fasting changed what clinicians understand about metabolic reset.
posts.build_brief: Format: Vertical reel cover (1080×1920px). The design mirrors the winner's news-article-overlay structure — a softly blurred background image (a warm, muted clinical or wellness setting, e.g. a naturopath's consultation room, morning light through a window, or a blurred close-up of whole foods) sits behind all text. Overlay a bold white sans-serif headline in uppercase, centred on the upper third of the canvas: "HOW FASTING REWIRES YOUR [METABOLIC] CLOCK" — with one meaningful word (e.g. "METABOLIC" or "CLOCK") highlighted in EHN green #39B54A italic, bold weight. Below, place a horizontal rounded banner bar in EHN green #39B54A (matching the black bar in the winner's layout) with white sans-serif body text: "PART OF OUR METABOLIC HEALTH SERIES". In the lower half, layer a second text zone in white or cream (semi-transparent overlay if needed for legibility) with a supporting line: "What research reveals about cellular renewal during fasting windows." Keep whitespace generous — mirror the winner's composition ratio (bold headline dominates upper third, banner sits mid-frame, supporting text sits lower). The overall feel is editorial and clinical, not stock-wellness. Green #39B54A is the unmistakable visual anchor. Background colour harmony: use a soft, warm neutral (cream, warm grey, or muted terracotta undertone) that complements green, never clashes. Include a small, unobtrusive EHN logo lockup in the bottom corner. Typography: Satoshi Bold for headline (95px+ relative scale), Satoshi Medium for supporting line (40px+ relative scale), white on muted background for maximum legibility.
posts.design_recipe: Format: Vertical reel cover (1080×1920px) with a softly blurred background image (a clinical or nature-based setting, rendered in warm, muted tones). Overlay structure mirrors a news-broadcast layout: bold uppercase headline in white sans-serif, centred in upper third, with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic; a horizontal rounded banner bar in EHN green #39B54A sits mid-frame with white body text; a supporting line in white or cream sits in lower half (semi-transparent overlay if needed). Whitespace ratio mirrors the winner's layout (headline-dominant upper third, banner mid, copy lower). Colour palette: EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor, complementary warm neutrals (cream, soft grey, muted terracotta) for background harmony — never purple, pink, orange, or bright red. Typography: Satoshi Bold (headline, 95px+ relative), Satoshi Medium (body, 40px+ relative). Small EHN logo lockup in corner. Overall feel: editorial, clinical, warm — grounded, not stock. On-canvas text must be >=36px minimum, body >=40px. Use this structure for any reel-cover topic: replace [headline], [banner text], and [supporting line] with topic-specific copy, maintain the green anchor and composition ratio.

posts.caption:
Over a decade of functional-medicine practice has shown us that fasting isn't just calorie restriction — it's a genuine shift in how your cells repair and renew themselves. New research into circadian-aligned eating windows reveals that timing, not just what you eat, shapes your metabolic health. We're diving into the science of fasting in our new series — no promises, no dogma, just evidence-based insights to help you understand what's happening during those rest periods. Interested in exploring fasting safely for your own health? Book a consultation with us — we'll map out what might work for your body.

#FunctionalMedicine #MetabolicHealth #IntermittentFasting #EvidenceBased #AdelaideHealthClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Discover how fasting rewires your metabolic health with our new series. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we explore evidence-based fasting strategies tailored to your unique body. Book an appointment today to learn more.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only post with minimal caption (approx. 15 words). Lead with casual, uplifting weekend mood statement ("Peach for Saturday 🫶🏽") followed by brand mention and hashtags. Warm, lifestyle-oriented copy with emoji accents. No image available to assess layout, colours, or visual hierarchy.
posts.angle: Weekend reset: small nutrition wins that feel good, not restrictive
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080×1080px), text-only, no image.

Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0 or similar warm neutral) to harmonize with EHN green and feel inviting + grounded.

Layout & hierarchy (faithfully mirror the winner's whitespace and density):
• Lead with a casual, uplifting weekend mood statement in the top third — e.g., "Green for Saturday 🫶🏽" — rendered in Satoshi BOLD, ~95px+ on canvas, with the single word "Green" in EHN green #39B54A italic or the entire hook in a warm tone to echo the weekend warmth.
• Mid section: a short subline (Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal #2B2B2B) that reframes the mood as a nutrition/wellness micro-win — e.g., "one good choice, no guilt."
• Lower third: business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, charcoal, ~40px, left-aligned or centred to match the winner's minimal, lifestyle brand placement.
• Whitespace-heavy, warm, intimate; no borders or graphic clutter — the green anchor and cream background do the work.

Colour anchors:
– Primary: EHN green #39B54A (the word "Green" or a subtle accent line)
– Secondary: warm cream background (#F5F3F0), charcoal text (#2B2B2B)
– Never: the competitor's exact palette; never pure black; never a cluttered visual.

Tone: warm, clinical-light, weekend-friendly, no supplement pitch — just a micro-moment of self-care language that speaks to a functional-medicine audience.

Hand this brief straight to Canva designer: text-only card, cream background, EHN green accent on one word, Satoshi throughout, minimal whitespace structure, grounded-natural feel.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Single square text-only card (1080×1080px).

Background: Soft, warm neutral (cream, pale sage, or charcoal — chosen to harmonize with #39B54A; never pure black, never competitor palette).

Layout (topic-agnostic, reusable shell):
• Top third: HEADLINE in Satoshi BOLD, ~95px+, with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic; conversational, uplifting weekend/lifestyle tone (e.g., "[mood word] for [day] 🫶🏽").
• Mid section: SUBLINE in Satoshi MEDIUM, ~48px, charcoal or complementary neutral, reframing the hook into a functional/wellness angle (e.g., "[benefit message], [no-guilt reassurance]").
• Lower third: BRAND LOCKUP — "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi MEDIUM, ~40px, charcoal, left-aligned or centred.
• Whitespace-heavy composition; no graphic clutter; green #39B54A as the sole visual anchor (via text or a minimal accent line).

Colour palette (locked, topic-flexible):
– Anchor: EHN green #39B54A (one word in headline or accent)
– Secondary: warm cream or soft sage (background), charcoal text
– Harmony: all colours chosen to feel grounded, clinical-but-warm, never competing with green

Type hierarchy:
– Headline: Satoshi BOLD (700), large (~95px+), one word in green accent
– Subline: Satoshi MEDIUM (500), smaller (~48px), charcoal
– Brand: Satoshi MEDIUM (500), ~40px

Feel: Minimal, text-led, lifestyle-warm, clinical-credible, Australian-natural; no stock wellness imagery; legibility floor: all text ≥36px minimum.

posts.visual_recipe: Centered text on soft muted blue-gray background. Top line in small caps ("HEALING ISN'T LINEAR"), main statement in large serif font (~60pt, medium weight) centered vertically, supporting italicized text below in medium serif (~36pt, lighter weight). posts.angle: Mental health and gut function are deeply connected—understanding this bidirectional relationship empowers smarter, whole-person healing.
posts.build_brief:
Create a 4-slide carousel on a warm cream background (harmonising with EHN green #39B54A). Each slide follows this structure:
- Slide number in a small dark charcoal pill-shaped badge, top-right corner.
- Top line in small caps (Satoshi Bold, ~48px, charcoal): a short phrase anchoring the theme.
- Main statement in large Satoshi Bold (~95px+, centred vertically, with ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic): the core insight.
- Supporting line in Satoshi Medium (~40px, lighter charcoal or sage tone, italicised, centred below main text): a softening or expansion of the idea.
- Ample whitespace between text blocks—do not crowd.
- All text centred, clean and minimal.

Slide 1: Top line "THE GUT-BRAIN AXIS" | Main text "Your *gut* isn't separate" (gut in green italic) | Supporting: "It talks to your mind, every day."
Slide 2: Top line "TWO-WAY STREET" | Main text "Stress *shapes* your microbiome" (shapes in green italic) | Supporting: "And your microbiome shapes your mood."
Slide 3: Top line "THE PATTERN" | Main text "Chronic inflammation *whispers*" (whispers in green italic) | Supporting: "Anxiety, brain fog, low mood—often rooted in the gut."
Slide 4: Top line "WHAT NOW?" | Main text "Healing *starts* with both" (starts in green italic) | Supporting: "Address stress + gut health together—they're one story."

Background: warm cream (off-white, never pure white, harmonious with green). Text colour: charcoal for body, green #39B54A for the one accent word. Include a small, unobtrusive icon or subtle illustration (e.g. a minimal gut outline, brain outline, or a gentle spiral suggesting connection) in soft sage or muted teal, placed near the supporting text or in the margin—nothing stock, keep it grounded and editorial. Logo small and unobtrusive, bottom-left corner. Close line bottom: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi Bold, ~32px.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable carousel template (topic-agnostic):

Canvas: 4-slide carousel, 1080 × 1350px per slide.
Background: warm cream or soft neutral (charcoal, soft sage, or muted teal also work), chosen to harmonise with green #39B54A anchor—never pure white, never pure black, never competitor's exact palette.

Layout per slide (consistent across all):
- Slide counter: small, dark pill-shaped badge, Satoshi Bold, ~24px, top-right corner (8–16px inset).
- Top line: small caps, Satoshi Bold, ~48px, charcoal or soft grey, centred horizontally. Generous margin top (~60–80px).
- Main statement: Satoshi Bold, ~95px+ (or up to ~120px if copy is very short), centred vertically and horizontally. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic; rest in charcoal or neutral dark tone.
- Supporting line: Satoshi Medium, ~40px, italicised, lighter charcoal or sage (not a clashing secondary colour), centred below main text. Generous whitespace between main and supporting.
- Illustration/icon: optional, subtle, placed in margin or near supporting text. Colours: soft sage, muted teal, or gentle green accent—nothing bright, nothing stock. Editorial, grounded.
- Logo: small, unobtrusive, bottom-left corner (~24–32px height).
- Close line (bottom): "Elemental Health & Nutrition", Satoshi Bold, ~32px, green #39B54A. Margin bottom ~20–30px.

Colour anchor: green #39B54A must be the primary visual anchor (the highlighted word in the main statement). Secondary colours (sage, teal, soft grey-blue, warm cream, charcoal, muted terracotta) must harmonise with green—never clash. Palette flex per post, but always green-anchored.

Type hierarchy: Satoshi BOLD for headlines (700), Satoshi MEDIUM for body (500). No serif, no Libre Caslon. All text ≥36px on body, ≥40px on body copy, ~95px+ on headline. Shorten copy if needed; never shrink font below legibility floor.

Aesthetic: clean, minimal, clinical-but-warm, with ample whitespace. Editorial, grounded, never stock-wellness. Soft, hopeful tone—introspective, not prescriptive.

posts.caption:
The gut–brain connection is one of the most profound discoveries in modern health science. Your microbiome isn't just digesting food—it's influencing your mood, anxiety, focus and emotional resilience.

When you're chronically stressed, your gut barrier weakens. When your gut is inflamed, your brain feels it. They talk to each other constantly.

This means healing isn't either-or. It's both. A functional medicine approach addresses stress regulation *and* gut health together, because they're inseparable.

If anxiety, brain fog or low mood are familiar, it's worth asking: what's happening in the gut? 🌿

Ready to explore the full picture? Let's talk.

#GutBrainAxis #FunctionalMedicine #MentalHealth #MicrobiomeMatters #HolisticHealth
posts.gmb_caption: The gut–brain connection is real—and it's central to lasting mental-health improvement. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we address stress, inflammation and microbiome health together. Ready to heal from the inside out? Book an appointment.

Center-bottom features an official-looking circular badge with "SIBO DOCTOR APPROVED" seal in white and lighter blue. Bottom-left corner has secondary branding logo. Layout is symmetrical and trust-focused, using medical/institutional authority cues through badge imagery and colour palette. No faces or photographs, solid colour background with branded elements only.
posts.angle: Most people with gut dysbiosis never get a proper functional-medicine diagnosis — validation and clarity matter.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: Single square card (1080×1080px).

Background: Warm cream (#F5F1ED) with a subtle sage-green geometric dot pattern overlay in the top-right corner (using EHN green #39B54A at 15% opacity to echo the winner's texture without replicating the navy).

Headline (top, centred, ~95px+, Satoshi Bold): "UNDERSTAND YOUR GUT" — with the word "GUT" rendered in EHN green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual hierarchy and mood.

Subheading (secondary text, ~50px, Satoshi Medium, charcoal): "Dysbiosis isn't a diagnosis. It's a clue."

Centre-bottom: A circular badge-style element (matching the winner's trust/authority aesthetic) — NOT a medical seal, but a grounded clinical marker. Outline in EHN green #39B54A (2–3px stroke), interior in soft white/cream, interior text in charcoal: "FUNCTIONAL MEDICINE APPROACH" (Satoshi Medium, ~32px, centre-aligned, two lines). This echoes the winner's badge authority without mimicking medical certification.

Bottom-left corner: Small EHN logo lockup (monochrome sage or charcoal, ~60px wide, unobtrusive).

Bottom-right: Brand close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, ~28px, charcoal.

Composition: Mirror the winner's symmetry and whitespace — headline dominates upper half, badge sits low and centred, flanked by breathing room. No photographs, no faces. Minimal, institutional but warm. The sage dot pattern and cream base create a soft, clinical feel aligned with EHN's grounded editorial voice.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: Single square card (1080×1080px).

Background: Warm neutral (cream, soft grey-white, or muted sage-cream hybrid) with a subtle geometric dot pattern overlay in the top-right corner — dots rendered in EHN green #39B54A at 15% opacity, creating texture without visual clutter.

Headline (top, centred, ~95px+, Satoshi Bold): "[MAIN WORD]" — with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic to anchor the visual and emotional centre.

Subheading (secondary text, ~50–55px, Satoshi Medium, charcoal): "[Supporting validation line, short phrase]"

Centre-bottom: Circular badge-style element (grounded clinical marker, NOT a medical seal). Outline in EHN green #39B54A (2–3px stroke), interior soft white/cream, text in charcoal (Satoshi Medium, ~32px, two lines, centre-aligned): "[Authority marker / clinical descriptor]". This element echoes the winner's trust cue and institutional feel.

Bottom-left corner: Small EHN logo lockup (monochrome sage or charcoal, ~60px wide, unobtrusive).

Bottom-right: Brand close line "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (Satoshi Medium, ~28px, charcoal).

Composition: Symmetrical, whitespace-rich (matching the winner's hierarchy). Headline dominates the upper half; badge sits low and centred, flanked by breathing room. No photography. Minimal, clinical-warm aesthetic. Colour palette: EHN green #39B54A as primary anchor; cream/white/muted sage as background; charcoal for secondary text; sage or soft grey-blue as optional tertiary (never pure black, never bright or clashing accents). The overall feel is editorial, grounded, and medically credible.
posts.on_image:
Headline: UNDERSTAND YOUR GUT (with "GUT" in green #39B54A italic)

Subheading: Dysbiosis isn't a diagnosis. It's a clue.

Badge text (two lines, centred): FUNCTIONAL MEDICINE APPROACH

Brand close line: Elemental Health & Nutrition
posts.caption:
Most people with gut dysbiosis never get a proper diagnosis — they're told "it's all in your head" or handed a probiotic and sent home.

Dysbiosis itself isn't the diagnosis. It's a *symptom* pointing to what's actually wrong: SIBO, leaky gut, dysbiosis, fungal overgrowth, or food sensitivities. Finding out which one matters, because the treatment is completely different.

At Elemental, we run the right functional tests — breath tests, stool analysis, food sensitivity markers — to understand *why* your gut isn't working. Once we know, we can actually fix it.

If you've been dismissed or left in the dark about your digestion, let's talk.

Ready to find answers? Book a consultation to discuss your gut health journey.

#GutHealth #Dysbiosis #FunctionalMedicine #SIBO #AdelaideFunctionalHealth
posts.gmb_caption: Most people with gut dysbiosis never get a proper functional-medicine diagnosis — just a label, no answers. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we run the right tests to understand what's actually wrong, so we can help you fix it. Book an appointment to start your gut health journey.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only carousel post (no image available). Multi-paragraph caption leading with contrarian headline hook ("Ten journals rejected...") establishing authority through research credentials. Approximately 120 words of body text in caption. Designed to be read as a carousel swipe-through narrative revealing suppressed research findings. Heavy hashtag footer targeting niche (EMF, building biology, sleep). The hook positions the creator as an outsider with forbidden/rejected knowledge—classic authority-register myth-busting angle in functional medicine space.
posts.angle: The sleep research journals won't publish — why gut health might be the missing link to your best sleep yet.
posts.build_brief:
FORMAT: Text-only carousel post (4 slides maximum). No image. Green-anchored, clinical authority register.

CANVAS & STRUCTURE:
- Square carousel format (1080 × 1080 px per slide).
- Background: Soft cream (#F5F3F0) throughout — warm, clinical, never white-bright or black.
- Whitespace-heavy layout matching the winner's narrative reveal structure: each slide introduces one idea in short, punchy blocks.
- Satoshi Bold headlines (~95px+, all-caps or title case) with ONE meaningful word in EHN green #39B54A italic or bold green accent.
- Body copy in Satoshi Medium, charcoal (#2C2C2C), 40–50px range for readability. Short paragraphs (1–2 lines max per block) to mirror the competitor's swipe-through pacing.
- Subtle visual anchor: a thin, 3–4px left border in EHN green #39B54A on each slide (consistent compositional anchor, no image needed).

SLIDE-BY-SLIDE COMPOSITION:
Slide 1 (Hook): 
- Headline centred or left-aligned, bold green word draws eye.
- Authority statement below (2–3 lines, body copy size).
- Whitespace below to invite scroll.
- Green left border present.

Slides 2–3 (Build):
- Short sub-headline per slide (Satoshi Bold, ~70–80px).
- Supporting line or stat (Satoshi Medium, ~45px).
- Whitespace padding to match Slide 1 rhythm.
- Green left border consistent.

Slide 4 (Close):
- Punchy CTA or clinician credential line (Satoshi Medium, ~50px).
- "Elemental Health & Nutrition" brand lockup (small, unobtrusive, bottom right, charcoal text).
- Green left border closes the suite.

COLOUR PALETTE:
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (left border, one word highlight per slide).
- Secondary: Soft cream background #F5F3F0 (calm, clinical, warm).
- Text: Charcoal #2C2C2C for body and most headlines; green italic accent on ONE meaningful word per slide.
- No competitor colours. No purple, bright pink, orange, neon, or clash. Green-anchored only.

TONE & FEEL:
- Authoritative, clinician-credible, myth-busting (mirror the winner's "forbidden knowledge" register).
- Editorial, grounded, never supplement-shilling.
- Whitespace and short copy blocks signal high-integrity research communication.
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE TEMPLATE (topic-agnostic, any EHN functional-medicine angle):

FORMAT: Text-only carousel suite (3–4 slides). Green-anchored, clinical authority register.

CANVAS: Square (1080 × 1080 px per slide). Background soft cream #F5F3F0 (or pale charcoal if topic mood demands warmer contrast). Never pure black, never competitor's exact palette.

COMPOSITIONAL ANCHOR:
- Thin left border (3–4px, EHN green #39B54A) on every slide — consistent visual thread, no image needed.
- Whitespace-heavy: short text blocks, breathing room between paragraphs (mirror myth-bust/research-reveal rhythm).
- Type hierarchy: Satoshi Bold (700) headlines at ~95px+ (Slide 1) and ~70–80px (subsequent slides); Satoshi Medium (500) body at 40–50px.

COLOUR SCHEME:
- Primary visual anchor: EHN green #39B54A (left border, one meaningful word italicised/accented per slide).
- Secondary palette: soft cream or pale neutral background + charcoal #2C2C2C text.
- Harmonising colours (if mood requires lift): sage, teal, warm grey-blue — never clash with green.

SLIDE STRUCTURE (Reusable):
1. **Hook slide**: [headline — one word in green italic] + [authority/contrarian statement] + whitespace.
2. **Build slide(s)**: [sub-headline] + [supporting stat or line] + whitespace.
3. **Build slide(s)**: [sub-headline] + [supporting insight] + whitespace.
4. **Close slide**: [CTA line or clinician credential] + "Elemental Health & Nutrition" lockup (small, charcoal, unobtrusive).

COPY DISCIPLINE:
- All on-screen text ≥36px (headlines ~95px+ Slide 1, ~70–80px subsequent, body 40–50px).
- Shorten copy ruthlessly — never shrink font below legibility floor.
- No image, no icon clutter — text and green-anchored border carry the authority.

VOICE & TONE:
- Myth-bust or research-reveal register (positioning EHN as credible outsider).
- Clinical, warm, Australian English, no supplement hyperbole.
- AHPRA/TGA-safe: no cure/treat/guarantee claims, no dosing.

This template allows the same clean, text-only carousel layout to be deployed across any functional-medicine topic (sleep, gut, hormones, inflammation, etc.) while maintaining EHN's green-anchored, high-integrity brand feel.
posts.on_image:
Slide 1: Sleep journals won't touch this | The gut–sleep connection most researchers ignore. | [Elemental Health & Nutrition logo, small bottom right]

Slide 2: Your microbiome | controls your melatonin | [green left border]

Slide 3: 70% of serotonin made in the gut — yet sleep medicine still treats the bedroom, not the belly. | [green left border]

Slide 4: Ready to connect the dots? | Book a sleep assessment — we look deeper. | Elemental Health & Nutrition | [green left border]
posts.caption:
Sleep troubles? Your GP might be looking in the wrong place. Most research on sleep hygiene stops at blue light and temperature — but your gut microbiome produces 70% of your serotonin, the neurotransmitter that makes melatonin possible. If your digestion is struggling, your sleep likely will too. This isn't suppressed research; it's just not the shiny headline. At Elemental, we trace sleep issues back to their root — often that means your gut health, not your pillow. Worth exploring? Let's chat about your sleep patterns and digestive health together.

Ready to uncover what's really affecting your sleep? Book a consultation with us.

#SleepHealth #GutBrain #FunctionalMedicine #AustralianClinic #SleepWellness
posts.gmb_caption: Sleep troubles often start in your gut, not your bedroom. At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Adelaide, we trace the real roots of poor sleep — including your microbiome and digestive health. Book an appointment to explore your sleep patterns with a clinician who looks deeper.

posts.visual_recipe: Outdoor daylight setting with a man in black casual wear standing on a sidewalk/path with manicured grass lawn and leafy green trees in background. He's gesturing while speaking directly to camera in a conversational, educational pose. Clean natural lighting, no on-image text overlay—designed as a talking-head reel format where caption text appears separately in the post copy.
posts.angle: Fasting isn't about restriction—it's about when your body actually *heals*.
posts.build_brief:
Format: Vertical reel/talking-head video (1080 × 1920px or TikTok/Instagram Reels proportion). 

Setting & composition: Outdoor daylight, natural lighting. A clinician (or relatable practitioner) in neutral, professional-casual wear (cream, charcoal, or muted tone) standing in a calm garden or park setting—manicured lawn, leafy trees, soft dappled light in background. Conversational posture, facing camera directly, gesturing naturally as if speaking to the viewer. NO on-image text overlay—the reel is a clean talking-head format; all caption text lives in the post copy below.

Colour palette: Green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor—introduce it as a subtle accent in the background (e.g. foliage, a soft sage plant pot, or a muted green garden element) or as a gentle colour grade/tint in the lighting (warm cream-and-green tone to the outdoor scene). Secondary colours: warm cream, soft sage, natural greens from foliage, charcoal soil/paths, soft grey-blue sky. Avoid pure black, bright colours, or the competitor's exact palette. The overall feel should be warm, grounded, clinician-calm—not clinical or sterile.

Whitespace & composition: Generous headroom above the speaker (rule of thirds, top third of frame occupied by background foliage/sky). Body occupies the middle and lower thirds. Slow, measured camera work—static or gentle zoom, no jump-cuts or hyperactive editing. The pacing should feel educational and thoughtful, not high-energy.

Typography: No text overlays on the reel itself. All copy is in the caption below (Instagram, Facebook, GBP). The reel is 100% visual; the voice is the educational hook.

Close/branding: Small, unobtrusive EHN logo appears briefly at the very end of the reel (final 1–2 seconds, bottom corner, white or cream, minimal opacity). No suburb name on the video itself.
posts.design_recipe:
Reusable Reel Template (Topic-Agnostic):

Format: Vertical talking-head video reel (1080 × 1920px). Outdoor daylight setting with natural, dappled lighting. Speaker in neutral, professional-casual wear, positioned in rule-of-thirds composition—conversational gesture, facing camera, framed against soft background foliage/landscape.

Colour palette foundation:
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A subtly woven into the background (foliage, garden element, or warm colour grade to the outdoor light).
- Secondary colours: warm cream, soft sage, natural greens, charcoal paths/soil, soft grey-blue sky.
- Avoid: pure black, bright/clashing colours, competitor's exact palette.
- Overall feel: warm, grounded, clinician-calm.

Composition & whitespace:
- Generous headroom (rule of thirds: top third = sky/foliage, middle/lower thirds = speaker).
- Static or gentle zoom camera movement; no jump-cuts.
- Measured, educational pacing—invites deep attention, not quick swipe-away.

Typography:
- ZERO on-image text overlays—clean talking-head format.
- All educational/hook copy lives in the caption below (Instagram, Facebook, GBP).
- Voice is the message; visuals anchor trust and approachability.

Branding & close:
- Small, unobtrusive EHN logo in final 1–2 seconds (bottom corner, white/cream, minimal opacity).
- No suburb name on the video itself; suburb keyword lives in GBP caption only.

This structure can be reused for any functional-medicine topic (digestion, sleep, stress, immune, hormones, etc.) where a conversational clinician talking directly to the viewer builds authority and trust.
posts.on_image: No on-image text. Reel is clean talking-head format with all copy in the caption. Visual hook only: speaker, outdoor daylight, natural foliage, conversational gesture, EHN green subtly anchored in background or colour grade. Logo brief at reel end (1–2 sec, bottom corner, minimal opacity).
posts.caption:
When you eat, your body digests. When you're not eating, it can *repair*.

Fasting isn't about starving yourself—it's about giving your gut, your cells, and your immune system space to reset. Most of us eat constantly, so we never really get that window.

If you're struggling with bloating, fatigue, or lingering inflammation, it might not be *what* you're eating—it might be *when*. A simple shift in timing can sometimes do more than any food swap.

That said, fasting isn't one-size-fits-all. Your history, your stress, your digestion all matter. We work out what rhythm makes sense *for you*.

Ready to rethink when—not just what?

**Elemental Health & Nutrition**

#FunctionalMedicine #GutHealth #IntermittentFasting #NutritionTiming #AdelaideClinic
posts.gmb_caption: Fasting can be a powerful reset tool—but it needs to fit *your* body, not a trend. In Eastwood, Adelaide, we work with you to find the eating (and fasting) rhythm that actually works. Book an appointment to explore whether timing could be your missing piece.

posts.visual_recipe: Candid, lifestyle photograph of a smiling man in a sage green and black North Face puffer jacket holding a small brown puppy in an indoor shopping/retail setting. Soft natural lighting, shallow depth of field with blurred background. Caption overlay with light emoji. High-engagement personal moment emphasizing relatability and warmth.
posts.angle: Real moments with clients: why our approach feels human, not clinical.
posts.build_brief:
Create a SINGLE SQUARE CARD (1080×1080px) in Canva using the winner's candid lifestyle layout.

COMPOSITION & WHITESPACE:
- Feature a warm, candid photograph of a client (or clinician + client) in the EHN clinic or wellness setting, smiling and at ease — soft natural or studio lighting, shallow depth of field, blurred background. The image fills the left 60–70% of the card. Think: genuine moment, not staged.
- Reserve right 30–40% as a clean white or soft cream breathing space for the overlay text.

COLOUR PALETTE (green-anchored, harmonizes with #39B54A):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A — use as a vertical accent bar or thick left border (width ~80–100px), or as a graphic shape (circle, organic blob) behind or beside the text.
- Secondary: soft cream (#F5F3F0) or white as the main card background; soft warm grey (#8B8680) for body text.
- No competitor's colours; no pure black or clashing pastels.

TEXT LAYOUT & HIERARCHY:
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~100–120px on 1080 canvas, roughly 5–7 words max): positioned in the cream breathing space, top-third or centre-right. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic or a complementary warm grey.
  Example layout: "Real. \[meaningful word in green\]. Moments." — very short, no word wrapping if possible.
- Subline/Caption (Satoshi Medium, ~50–60px, 1–2 short lines): sit directly below the headline in warm grey. A brief, warm clinician voice observation (e.g., "Why our clinic feels like home").
- Small brand lockup (EHN logo, 60–80px) + "Elemental Health & Nutrition" label (Satoshi Medium, ~36px, warm grey or green) sit bottom-right, subtle but present.

FEEL:
- Warm, human, candid — matches the winner's relatability and soft smile. Editorial photography, not stock imagery.
- Shallow depth of field mimics the winner's approachability.
- Green accent bar or shape anchors the eye and signals EHN instantly.
- Plenty of whitespace, minimal copy — let the image and green accent do the work.

DELIVER TO DESIGNER AS:
"Single square card with candid clinic/wellness photo (left 60–70%), cream/white breathing space (right 30–40%). EHN green vertical accent bar or shape. Bold headline (~100px, one word in green italic), subline (~50px, warm grey), tiny logo + close line bottom-right. Soft natural lighting, shallow depth of field, warm-toned and human."
posts.design_recipe:
REUSABLE CANDID LIFESTYLE CARD TEMPLATE — Topic-Agnostic

FORMAT: Single square card (1080×1080px)

LAYOUT & COMPOSITION:
- Left 60–70%: warm, candid lifestyle photograph (clinic setting, wellness moment, clinician + client interaction, soft natural/studio lighting, shallow depth of field, blurred background). Image does the heavy emotional lifting.
- Right 30–40%: clean, breathing white or soft cream space for text overlay.

COLOUR PALETTE (EHN green-anchored):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A as vertical accent bar (~80–100px width, left edge or behind text), or as an organic shape (circle, blob, underline) adjacent to headline.
- Secondary: soft cream (#F5F3F0) or white card background.
- Body text: soft warm grey (#8B8680) or charcoal for readability.
- No pure black, no clashing pastels, no competitor's palette.

TEXT HIERARCHY & PLACEMENT:
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~100–120px relative scale on 1080, 5–7 words MAX): top-third or centre-right of cream space. ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic or complementary accent.
- Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~50–60px relative scale, 1–2 short lines): directly below headline, warm grey tone, clinician-warm voice.
- Brand close (logo lockup 60–80px + "Elemental Health & Nutrition" label, Satoshi Medium ~36px, warm grey or green): bottom-right, minimal presence.

FEEL & TONE:
- Candid, warm, human, relatable — genuine moment, not staged.
- Editorial photography aesthetic (not stock wellness clichés).
- Green as the unmistakable visual anchor.
- Ample whitespace, minimal copy — image-led storytelling.
- Clinical-but-warm, grounded Australian natural light.

USE THIS SHELL FOR: personal stories, client moments, behind-the-scenes, clinic culture, clinician perspective, any topic that benefits from candid human connection.
posts.on_image:
Headline: "Real \[meaningful word in green italic\] Moments" (or similar 5–7 word structure)
Subline: "Why our clinic feels like home" (or related 1–2 line clinician observation)
Close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition"
posts.caption:
This is what it looks like when health feels personal, not transactional. We love when clients walk through the door already smiling — it tells us we're doing something right. Your wellness journey doesn't have to feel clinical; it just has to feel *real*. 

Ready to feel the difference? Book a free discovery call with us today.

#FunctionalMedicine #AdelhealthCare #RealMoments #WellnessJourney
posts.gmb_caption: At Elemental Health & Nutrition in Eastwood, Adelaide, we believe your health journey should feel warm and human, not clinical. Every appointment is a real conversation with a clinician who listens. Book your discovery call today and experience the difference.

posts.visual_recipe: High-contrast studio portrait against a dark charcoal/black textured wall backdrop. Woman in fitted grey/slate ribbed long-sleeve dress with ruching detail at center, minimal gold accessories (bracelets/rings), hand on hip in confident power pose. Clean, polished aesthetic with concrete flooring. Minimal text overlay—brand mention and emoji callouts only (no on-image text apart from caption).
posts.angle: Functional medicine is about meeting your body where it is, not where wellness trends say it should be.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single square card (1080×1080px).

Layout & composition: High-contrast studio portrait of a person (clinician or real patient) shot from waist-up against a soft sage or warm charcoal textured wall backdrop (muted, never pure black). Subject dressed in neutral, polished professional attire (soft grey, cream, or charcoal tones) with minimal, natural accessories—no jewellery required. Pose: relaxed, grounded confidence (arm resting naturally or hand on hip), feet firmly planted, direct gaze. Clean concrete or neutral flooring visible at base.

Background & colour: The backdrop wall is a soft muted charcoal or sage-grey (warm undertone, never black). This allows the subject to read clearly and creates calm, clinical authority without coldness.

Text placement: Minimal on-image text overlay. NO lengthy copy on the card itself—only a single headline (ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic, remainder in charcoal bold Satoshi) placed at the centre-top or top-third of the card. Optionally, a short brand tagline or emoji callout (2–3 words max) at the bottom in charcoal.

Green #39B54A anchor: Use green as a subtle border frame (1–2% of the card edge), a thin rule beneath the headline, or as the colour of that single meaningful word. Green must be visible and intentional but not dominating—it feels like a clinical accent, not a full-bleed background.

Legibility: Headline ~95px (bold Satoshi, charcoal body text in grey/charcoal, green accent word ~95px italic). No body copy on card; all narrative lives in the caption.

Overall feel: Editorial, grounded, clinical-but-warm. The portrait carries the message; the minimal text acts as a clinician's quiet endorsement, not a hard sell.
posts.design_recipe:
Canvas: single square card (1080×1080px).

Reusable layout: High-contrast studio or editorial portrait (waist-up, subject facing camera) centred against a soft, muted textured wall backdrop in complementary neutrals (sage, warm grey, soft charcoal—never pure black, never bright colours). Subject in neutral professional attire, minimal or no jewellery, grounded pose (natural arm placement or light confident gesture). Clean flooring or neutral base visible.

Colour palette: 
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A (used as one highlighted word in the headline, a thin rule beneath, or a delicate border frame—never as full-bleed background).
- Secondary: Soft muted charcoal, sage, or warm grey for the backdrop (ensures contrast with subject while staying clinical and calm).
- Tertiary: Charcoal (CMYK 0/0/0/80–100) for body text and brand close line.

Text hierarchy:
- Headline: Satoshi Bold, ~95px, charcoal body + one meaningful word in green #39B54A italic, placed centre-top or top-third.
- Optional callout/tagline: Satoshi Medium, ~36–40px, charcoal, 2–3 words max, placed bottom-centre.
- Brand close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green text, Satoshi Medium, ~36px, bottom-right corner).

Composition: Portrait occupies 65–75% of canvas, whitespace/neutral ground 25–35%. Minimal on-image text preserves the editorial impact of the photograph. All narrative voice and CTA belong in the caption.

Feel: Grounded, editorial, clinician-credible. The portrait is the hero; the text is restraint and professionalism.
posts.on_image: Headline: "Your body *knows* what it needs" (where "knows" is green #39B54A italic, bold Satoshi ~95px, charcoal remainder) | Optional tag: "Just ask." (Satoshi Medium, ~36px, charcoal, placed bottom-centre) | Brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green, Satoshi Medium, ~36px, bottom-right)
posts.caption:
Your body isn't trying to sabotage you—it's sending signals. In functional medicine, we listen. That might mean slowing down when the world says speed up, or nourishing differently when the trend feels wrong for your system.

There's no one-size-fits-all. There's just your body, your story, and a clinician who's trained to help you decode what's really going on.

Ready to listen to what yours is telling you? Let's start a conversation.

#FunctionalMedicine #WellnessWithoutTrends #ListeningToYourBody #HealthAsIt #ClinicalNutrition
posts.gmb_caption: Functional medicine isn't about following a trend—it's about understanding what your body actually needs. If you're ready to work with a clinician who listens and personalises your care, we'd love to meet you in Eastwood, Adelaide. Book an appointment today.
